finding: Independently sampled features from each location enable veridical perception of bound objects only when selection precision is sufficient; binding errors arise when independently sampled features originate from different objects.
Scene-based three-dimensional properties facilitate visual search similarly to image-based two-dimensional properties, supporting representation of scene-based properties at preattentive levels.
Higher social position predicts increased unethical behavior across multiple contexts; this effect is mediated by more favorable attitudes toward self-interest.
Uncertainty about another's preference for the self increases attraction more than high preference; uncertainty increases thought about the other person, which mediates attraction.
Affect misattribution comprises three separable processes: affective response to true cause, affective response to apparent cause, and confusion of apparent for real source.
Exposure to prosocial content increases helping and cooperative behavior; this effect is mediated by accessibility of prosocial thoughts.
Sexualized women are perceived analytically (like objects, showing no inversion effect) whereas sexualized men are perceived configurally (like persons, showing inversion effect).
Subliminal exposure to evaluative symbols (approach vs. avoidance cues) enhances or impairs cognitive performance via nonconscious processes outside awareness.
Prejudice arises from perceived ideological dissimilarity regardless of political group membership; liberals and conservatives show symmetrical preference for similar-minded others.
Facial expression dominates approachability judgments when integrating face and body cues; incongruent body expression is less accurately recognized when paired with incongruent facial expression.
Irrelevant numerical magnitude automatically biases duration reproduction; the effect direction depends on whether the magnitude is stimulus-linked or response-linked.
Probability judgments bias toward outcomes in larger categories regardless of objective probability or category relevance; category size influences risk perception and decision behavior.
Evaluations of others recursively transfer to the evaluator; people who like others are liked more and vice versa via higher-order propositional inferences.
Mindfulness meditation reduces sunk-cost bias by shifting temporal focus away from past and future and by reducing negative affect.
Consumers track and construe experience categories; they avoid eliminating positive experience categories but accept eliminating negative ones to feel progress.
Reproductive hormone elevation increases intergroup bias toward men perceived as physically formidable, extending beyond race to minimal group categories.
Odd numbers are associated with masculinity and even numbers with femininity; this association influences gender judgments of arbitrarily paired neutral stimuli.
Hierarchical social structures are processed more fluently than egalitarian ones; processing ease leads to greater liking and preference for hierarchy.
Personality and lifestyle factors (procrastination, conscientiousness, extroversion) predict disambiguation of ambiguous temporal expressions toward different conceptual metaphors.
Warm ambient or tactile conditions increase social proximity, concrete language, and relational focus; temperature grounds abstract social concepts.
